name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لإجراءات تسجيل البيانات وإصدار بطاقة كفاءة استهلاك الطاقة في المركبات والإطارات
summary:
This procedural technical regulation (م.إ-155-16-10-01-01), published March 2022, establishes the data registration procedures and licensing requirements for issuing the Energy Consumption Efficiency Card (energy label) for vehicles and tires in Saudi Arabia. It covers: registration of accredited laboratories to issue test reports (2-year license); licensing conditions for establishments to use the energy label (12-month license per model); application steps including submission of Gulf conformity certificates and accredited test reports; vehicle manufacturer supply plan registration; fees (2,000 SAR per tire model, 5,000 SAR per vehicle model, 15,000–150,000 SAR for supply plans); violations and penalties administered by the Ministry of Commerce; and market surveillance by SASO and Ministry of Commerce. No HS code annex is included in this regulation.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Paints and Varnishes
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للدهانات (الأصباغ) والورنيشات
summary:
This technical regulation (Second Edition, Amendment 2, published in Official Gazette on 04/11/2022) governs paints (pigments) and varnishes used in residential, commercial and public buildings in Saudi Arabia. It establishes mandatory requirements covering: environmental performance classification (VOC content limits, prohibition/limits on heavy metals, aromatic hydrocarbons, halogenated hydrocarbons and formaldehyde); packaging requirements (no lead/heavy metals, recycling symbol, tamper-proof); metrology requirements (SI units, GSO OIML R 87); and comprehensive labeling requirements. Suppliers must obtain a conformity certificate through Type 3 assessment per ISO/IEC 17067 from a SASO-accepted CAB, issue a Supplier Declaration of Conformity, maintain a 10-year technical file, and obtain a separate SASO license to use the Environmental Performance Efficiency Label. Regulatory authorities at customs ports and factories conduct document verification and random sampling; market surveillance authorities monitor the market and enforce compliance. Non-compliance can result in product withdrawal, recall, and penalties under the Anti-Commercial Fraud Law. Environmental performance classification requirements have a 2-year grace period from regulation approval date.

---

name_en: Gulf Technical Regulation for Low Voltage Electrical Appliances and Equipment – List (2)
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية الخليجية للأجهزة والمعدات الكهربائية منخفضة الجهد – القائمة (2)
summary:
TC143307-01 is the Gulf Technical Regulation for Low Voltage Electrical Devices and Equipment – List (2), issued by GSO on 06 August 2014. This document enumerates 13 categories of domestic electrical appliances (with corresponding HS codes) that are subject to the conformity assessment procedure defined in Annex 4 of the parent regulation under Article 20. Products covered include fans, refrigerators, washing machines, food processors, toasters, hair dryers, heating apparatus, microwave ovens, electric ovens, water heaters, electric irons, plugs/sockets/chargers, and air conditioners.

---

name_ar: اللائحة العامة لعلامة الجودة السعودية
summary:
This is the General Regulation for the Saudi Quality Mark (4th edition, approved January 2023, published March 2023). It is an administrative/procedural regulation issued by SASO that establishes the general rules, conditions, and procedures for granting, maintaining, expanding, renewing, suspending, withdrawing, and cancelling licenses to use the Saudi Quality Mark on products. The regulation does not cover specific product categories or HS codes - it serves as the overarching framework applicable to all products for which specific granting procedures (إجراء المنح) are developed. The Quality Mark is exclusively owned by SASO (trademark registration no. 143205020). The certification scheme follows ISO/IEC 17065 requirements. A three-year renewable license is granted following initial audit and product testing. Ongoing surveillance includes periodic audits and random market sampling. Two oversight committees are established: the Neutrality Committee (advisory, meeting at least annually) and the Review and Decision Committee (operational decision-making). Financial costs include application fees (SAR 3,000), document review (SAR 2,500/person-day), auditing (SAR 3,000/auditor-day), quality mark usage rights (SAR 15,000-25,000 for optional products; SAR 15,000 for mandatory products), plus travel, accommodation, and testing costs borne by the applicant facility.

---

name_en: General Technical Regulation for Energy Efficiency
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية العامة لكفاءة استهلاك الطاقة
summary:
The General Technical Regulation for Energy Efficiency (م.إ-٠٥-٠٤-١٦-١٥٦), first issued in 2016 and last amended in October 2023, establishes mandatory energy efficiency requirements for products approved by the SASO Board of Directors. Currently covering 13 product categories (air conditioners, refrigerators/freezers, washing machines, lighting products parts 1-3, electric motors, new vehicle fuel economy, tyres, clothes dryers, water heaters, and dishwashers), the regulation requires suppliers to obtain a model energy efficiency certificate (Type 1a per ISO/IEC 17067) and an energy label use license from SASO or an accepted body. Suppliers must submit test reports from SASO-registered ISO 17025 laboratories, a Supplier Declaration of Conformity, technical files, and evidence of an effective quality management system. Certificates and licenses are valid for one year and renewable. Market surveillance authorities and regulatory bodies may conduct random product sampling. Non-compliant products are subject to withdrawal, recall, and penalties under anti-commercial fraud regulations.

---

name_ar: اللائحة العامة لنماذج المطابقة
summary:
The General Regulation for Conformity Models (م.إ-٠٣-٠١-٠٥-٢٣-١٩٤-٠٣) is a SASO framework regulation approved on 28 September 2023 and published in the Official Gazette on 17 November 2023. It does not apply to a specific product category but rather defines the standardized conformity assessment models to be referenced by all SASO product-specific technical regulations. The regulation establishes eight conformity models: three first-party self-declaration models (Type S, S1, S2) and five third-party certification models (Type 1a Type Approval, Type 1b Shipment Conformity, Type 2 Market Product Conformity, Type 3 Production Process Conformity, Type 4 Production Process + Market Sampling, Type 5 Full Quality Assurance). It is based on ISO/IEC 17067 and aligns with WTO TBT Agreement principles. Type 5 is equivalent to the Saudi Quality Mark license and requires ISO 9001 QMS compliance. All models require a Supplier Declaration of Conformity and technical documentation retained for 10 years.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Communications and Information Technology Devices
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لأجهزة الاتصالات وتقنية المعلومات
summary:
This technical regulation, issued by SASO in coordination with the Communications, Space and Technology Commission (CITC), establishes mandatory requirements for all communications and information technology devices in Saudi Arabia (excluding military and security devices). It covers a broad range of ICT equipment including smartphones, tablets, laptops, base stations, satellite terminals, maritime/aviation communications, radar, broadcasting equipment, and more. Suppliers must ensure devices meet essential and technical requirements including electrical safety, electromagnetic compatibility, radiation exposure limits, frequency spectrum protection, and network interoperability. All devices require Type 1a conformity assessment (type approval) by an accepted CAB per ISO/IEC 17067. Products listed in Annex 3 (mobile phones, laptops, smart watches, TVs, gaming equipment, charging cables, desktop PCs) additionally require IECEE NCB certificates. Annex 2 mandates USB Type-C standardization for chargers of smartphones and electronic devices by January 2025, and laptops by April 2026. Labeling must be in Arabic and include supplier/manufacturer information, model, country of origin, usage warnings, and frequency band information for wireless devices. Violations are subject to penalties under the Anti-Commercial Fraud Law and CITC regulations.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Amusement Parks, Games and Devices
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لأجهزة وألعاب المدن الترفيهية
summary:
This Technical Regulation (ID: م.إ-١٦٥-١٨-٠٤-٠٢), approved in 2018 and amended in 2023, establishes mandatory safety and operational requirements for amusement rides, devices, and games installed permanently or temporarily in amusement parks in Saudi Arabia. It covers design, manufacture, installation, operation, maintenance, and inspection. The regulation applies to products under HS codes 8903, 9504, 9506, and 9508. Conformity assessment follows Type 3 per ISO/IEC 17067, requiring a certified Product Safety Management System and Type Approval from an accepted body, plus a mandatory inspection certificate from an ISO 17020-accredited inspection body before public use. Inspection certificates are renewed annually for devices and every 3 years for rides. Key obligations are imposed on suppliers, device/city owners, city management, and safety officers. Twenty-nine referenced Saudi standards cover all product sub-types including playground equipment, water slides, inflatable equipment, go-karts, bumper boats, trampoline courts, and climbing structures. The regulation does not apply to ice cities, snow vehicles, or ice-skating games.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Water Efficiency Products
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لأدوات ترشيد استهلاك المياه
summary:
This technical regulation issued by SASO (regulation ID م.إ-١٥٦-١٦-٠٤-٠٦, first edition with amendment 1, published 2020) establishes mandatory water efficiency requirements for water-saving plumbing products sold in Saudi Arabia, including taps/mixers (public, private/kitchen, shower, bidet/shattaf), western and eastern flush toilets, urinals, flow regulators, and flushing cisterns. Products must meet maximum flow rate limits specified in Annex 1 and must carry the SASO Water Efficiency Label. Conformity is assessed via Type 1a (Type Approval) per ISO/IEC 17067, requiring submission of a technical file with laboratory test reports (from ISO/IEC 17025 accredited laboratories) and a Supplier Declaration of Conformity to SASO or an accepted body. A Water Efficiency Type Certificate and a separate label use license are issued, both valid for one year and renewable. Referenced standards include SASO 1473, SASO 1257, SASO 1477, SASO EN 246, SASO 2655, and NSF 61 (reference only). Non-compliant products are subject to withdrawal, recall, and penalties under the Commercial Fraud regulations.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Electric Personal Mobility Devices (Scooter)
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لوسائل التنقل الفردية الكهربائية (السكوتر)
summary:
This is the second edition of the SASO Technical Regulation for Electric Personal Mobility Devices (Scooters), approved by the SASO Board of Directors in September 2023 and published in the Official Gazette in October 2023. It applies to electric personal mobility scooters, their batteries, and chargers. The regulation mandates compliance with 12 referenced SASO/ISO/IEC standards covering electrical safety, battery safety, and charging equipment. Key requirements include: maximum speed of 25 km/h, mandatory front/rear lights and brakes, reflectors, audible warning device, instruction manual with safety warnings, and an effective quality management system at the manufacturer. Conformity is assessed via Type 3 per ISO/IEC 17067, requiring certification from a SASO-accepted body through factory audits and product safety management system approval. Suppliers must maintain a technical file including a risk assessment and Supplier Declaration of Conformity for at least 10 years. Scooters classified as toys must also comply with the Gulf Toys Regulation. The regulation includes provisions for market surveillance, violations, penalties, and transitional periods of 180 days for application and 365 days for suppliers to correct market status.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Small Photovoltaic Solar Energy Systems
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لأنظمة الطاقة الشمسية الكهروضوئية الصغيرة
summary:
This technical regulation (Second Edition, approved August 2019, published March 2020) establishes mandatory safety and conformity requirements for small photovoltaic solar energy systems and their components sold in Saudi Arabia. It covers PV modules, inverters, cables and conductors, switchgear and control equipment, and interface protection devices (HS codes: 8504, 8541, 8544). Suppliers must register products with SASO and obtain a national conformity certificate aligned with the IECEE CB Scheme through an accepted conformity assessment body. Manufacturers must have ISO 9001 and ISO 14001 certifications. Products must comply with an extensive list of IEC, SASO, EN, and UL standards covering design qualification, safety, EMC, and performance. Suppliers must maintain a technical file, submit a Supplier Declaration of Conformity, and cooperate with regulatory and market surveillance authorities. Transitional provisions allow 6 months for compliance and 1 year for trading of non-compliant products from publication date.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Simple Pressure Vessels
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لأوعية الضغط البسيطة
summary:
This SASO Technical Regulation for Simple Pressure Vessels (issued June 2022, published September 2022) establishes mandatory safety and conformity requirements for welded simple pressure vessels designed to contain air or nitrogen under internal pressure exceeding 0.5 bar. The regulation covers steel, aluminium, and aluminium alloy vessels with maximum operating pressure up to 30 bar and PS×V up to 3000 bar*litre, operating between -50°C and 300°C (steel) or 100°C (aluminium). Excluded are nuclear, marine/aviation propulsion devices, and SFDA-regulated products. Suppliers must meet technical, metrological, environmental, and packaging requirements. Conformity assessment is two-tiered: Type 1a (Type Approval) for PS×V ≤ 50 bar*litre; Type 3 (Type Approval + Product Safety Management System certification) for PS×V > 50 bar*litre. Both routes require certificates from SASO-accepted conformity assessment bodies. Vessels must bear required markings (PS, Tmax, Tmin, capacity, manufacturer identification) in Arabic. Non-compliant products are subject to withdrawal, recall, and penalties under Saudi commercial fraud regulations. A transitional period of 180 days applies for implementation, with 365 days for suppliers to correct their market status.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Cableway Installations
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لتركيبات العربات المعلقة
summary:
This technical regulation establishes mandatory safety and conformity requirements for cableway installations designed to transport persons in Saudi Arabia, including cable cars, chairlifts and drag lifts, covering new installations, modifications requiring new authorization, subsystems and safety components (HS code 8428). The regulation defines essential requirements for design, construction, commissioning and operation covering structural integrity, safety devices, braking systems, drive systems, vehicles and operational safety. Suppliers must obtain a Type 3 conformity certificate per ISO/IEC 17067 from an SASO-accepted body, operate a certified Product Safety Management System, obtain a pre-commissioning inspection certificate from an ISO 17020-accredited inspection body, and maintain technical documentation for 10 years. Fourteen referenced standards (EN/SASO EN series) cover all aspects from ropes and structures to electrical equipment and fire protection. The regulation was approved by SASO Board Decision No. 192 dated 26/12/1444H and published in the Official Gazette on 09/02/1445H, with a 180-day implementation period and 365-day market correction period.

---

name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لتصنيف مراكز صيانة وإصلاح المركبات
summary:
This SASO Technical Regulation (2nd Edition, approved March 2022) establishes a mandatory star-based classification system (1 to 4 stars) for all vehicle maintenance and repair facilities operating in Saudi Arabia, excluding military and security sector facilities. Facilities are first classified by activity type (Categories A–F covering full-service centers, mechanical repair, bodywork, specialized systems, combined services, and quick-service centers). Star ratings are determined by a scoring formula across six criteria: location/services, health & safety, documentation, spare parts management, staff training/competency, and equipment. Mandatory safety requirements per SASO-1334 apply from 2-star upward. Conformity assessment for 1-star requires only self-declaration and SASO document review; 2-star and above require on-site inspection by an SASO-accepted inspection body with annual surveillance. Classification certificates are valid for 3 years. Non-compliance can result in suspension, downgrade, or revocation of the classification certificate.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Ornaments and Accessories
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لحلي الزينة والإكسسوارات
summary:
This technical regulation issued by SASO covers ornaments and accessories (حلي الزينة والإكسسوارات) made from metals, plastics, glass, or textiles. It excludes precious metals, jewelry, plating, and their workpieces. The regulation establishes mandatory general, technical, chemical, environmental, and packaging requirements. Key chemical requirements include limits on heavy metals (lead, cadmium, nickel). Products must undergo Type 1a conformity assessment (Type Approval) per ISO/IEC 17067 through a SASO-accepted conformity assessment body. Suppliers must maintain a technical file with a Supplier Declaration of Conformity and risk assessment document. Arabic labeling with supplier details, origin, and safety warnings is mandatory. The regulation was approved on 30 June 2022 and published in the Official Gazette on 23 September 2022, with a 180-day implementation period and a 365-day correction period for suppliers.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Front, Rear and Side Barriers for Trucks and Trailers
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للحواجز الأمامية والخلفية والجانبية للشاحنات والمقطورات
summary:
This technical regulation (Second Edition, approved June 2022, published September 2022) establishes mandatory safety requirements for front, rear and side underrun protective barriers for trucks, trailers and semi-trailers (categories N2, N3, O3, O4) with maximum weight exceeding 3.5 tons in Saudi Arabia. It excludes off-road trucks, military vehicles, and vehicles where barrier installation is impractical. Suppliers must obtain type approval certificates through SASO or accredited conformity assessment bodies per the Type 1a procedure of ISO/IEC 17067. Barriers must comply with Saudi standards SASO 2955, 2956, 2957, and reflective markings per SASO 2913. Trucks and trailers without compliant barriers may not obtain or renew vehicle registration. Non-compliant products are subject to withdrawal, recall, and penalties under the commercial fraud law.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Lubricating and Greasing Oils
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لزيوت التزييت والتشحيم
summary:
This is the second edition of the Saudi Technical Regulation for Lubricating and Greasing Oils, approved by SASO Board of Directors in September 2023 and published in the Official Gazette in October 2023. It covers a wide range of lubricating oils including engine oils for gasoline and diesel vehicles, base oils, brake fluids, automatic transmission fluids, hydraulic oils, gear oils, greases, insulating oils, compressor oils, turbine oils, CLP and CL lubricants, two-stroke engine oils, heat treatment oils, and refrigerant compressor oils. Products covered fall under HS codes 2710, 2713, 3403, and 3819. Suppliers must comply with 40 referenced standards listed in Annex 1, obtain a conformity certificate through Type 3 assessment (per ISO/IEC 17067) from a SASO-accepted body, maintain an ISO 14001-certified environmental management system, and comply with labeling requirements per GSO 1810. The conformity assessment involves Product Safety Management System evaluation and approval. Transition period allows 180 days for implementation and 365 days for market correction after Official Gazette publication.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Machinery Safety - Part 4: Sawing and Cutting Machines
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لسلامة الآلات - الجزء الرابع: آلات النشر والتقطيع
summary:
This is the Technical Regulation for Machinery Safety - Part 4: Sawing and Cutting Machines, issued by SASO (Board Meeting No. 188, approved 2023-01-05, published in Official Gazette 2023-02-17). It covers sawing and cutting machines, related safety components, and interchangeable equipment, including hand-held and hand-guided variants, falling under HS codes 8420, 8439, 8444, 8461, 8464, and 8465. Suppliers must conduct risk assessments, design and manufacture machines to meet essential health and safety requirements detailed in Annex 2, and obtain Type Approval certification (Type 1a per ISO/IEC 17067) from a SASO-accepted conformity assessment body. A Supplier Declaration of Conformity and technical documentation must accompany products. Safety components and interchangeable equipment for already-certified machine models supplied by the manufacturer or legal representative are exempt from separate conformity assessment. Labelling and instructions must be in Arabic. Non-compliant products may be withdrawn from market, and violations are subject to penalties under Saudi anti-commercial fraud regulations. The regulation applies 180 days from publication, with a 365-day transition period for suppliers to rectify their market status.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Machinery Safety - Part 1: Hand-held and/or Hand-guided Machinery
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لسلامة الآلات الجزء الأول: الآلات المحمولة و/أو الموجهة باليد
summary:
This technical regulation establishes mandatory health and safety requirements for hand-held and/or hand-guided machinery, their safety components, and interchangeable equipment placed on the Saudi market. It covers machinery under HS codes 8433 and 8467. Suppliers must conduct risk assessments, comply with general and specific safety requirements detailed in Annex 2, and reference 82 product-specific Saudi standards listed in Annex 1. Conformity assessment is performed via Type Approval (Type 1a per ISO/IEC 17067) by an SASO-accepted body. Required documentation includes a Type Approval Certificate, Supplier Declaration of Conformity, technical file, and instructions manual. The regulation was adopted at SASO Board meeting No. 176 on 09/07/2020 and published in the Official Gazette on 11/09/2020, with a six-month transition period for compliance and prohibition on non-compliant products after one year from publication.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Machine Safety - Part 3: Lifting Equipment
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لسلامة الآلات - الجزء الثالث: معدات الرفع
summary:
This regulation establishes mandatory safety requirements for lifting equipment and accessories sold in Saudi Arabia, covering hoists (HS 8425), cranes (HS 8426), fork-lift trucks (HS 8427), and other lifting/handling machinery (HS 8428), along with their safety components and interchangeable equipment. Excluded are lifting equipment with speed ≤0.15 m/s, cable car systems, military equipment, mechanical escalators, and temporary construction site elevators. Suppliers must conduct risk assessments, comply with 172 referenced SASO/GSO/ISO/EN standards, obtain Type 1a conformity certificates from accepted bodies per ISO/IEC 17067, and have an effective quality management system (ISO 9001). Used lifting equipment and newly installed equipment require inspection certificates from ISO/IEC 17020-accredited bodies accepted by SASO. Periodic inspections are mandatory during service life. Products must be labeled in Arabic with maximum working load, and instructions must be provided in Arabic. Violations are subject to penalties under the Anti-Commercial Fraud Law.

---

name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للصهاريج – الجزء الأول: صهاريج نقل المنتجات البترولية
summary:
This regulation (م-إ-١٧١-١٩-٦-٠٣), approved by SASO Board in June 2019 and published in the Official Gazette in August 2019, establishes mandatory requirements for the design, manufacture, inspection and testing of petroleum products transport tanks (tank trucks and tank trailers) in Saudi Arabia. Suppliers must obtain Type Approval (Type 1a per ISO/IEC 17067) from an SASO-accepted conformity assessment body and must hold a valid quality management system (ISO 9001). Tanks undergo pre-service and annual periodic inspections by an accepted body, which affixes a QR-coded inspection sticker. Referenced Saudi standards include SASO-2288, SASO-1285, SASO-1287, SASO-2809, SASO-1284, and SASO-469. No HS codes are listed in the regulation. Compliance is required within 6 months of publication; non-compliant products may not be traded after 2 years from publication date.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Child Restraint Systems and Strollers
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لعربات وكراسي الأمان للأطفال
summary:
This technical regulation issued by SASO (Board Meeting No. 184, dated 28/08/1443H / 31/03/2022, published in Official Gazette 19/10/1443H) establishes mandatory safety and quality requirements for wheeled child conveyances (strollers, prams, pushchairs) and child restraint systems (car seats) sold in Saudi Arabia. The regulation covers chemical requirements (restrictions on hazardous substances in materials and paints), mechanical requirements (wheel stability, braking, child restraint harnesses, folding lock safety, structural integrity), installation requirements for car seats (classified into four age/weight categories per Annex 2), metrological requirements (SI units), environmental requirements, packaging requirements, and labeling requirements (Arabic language, mandatory warnings). Conformity assessment is conducted via Type 1a procedure per ISO/IEC 17067, requiring type approval certification from a SASO-accepted third-party conformity assessment body, accompanied by a Supplier Declaration of Conformity and a risk assessment document. Products holding the Saudi Quality Mark or equivalent are deemed compliant. The regulation applies within 180 days of publication, with a 365-day grace period for market correction.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Auto Spare Parts
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لقطع غيار السيارات
summary:
This technical regulation (م.إ-١٦٢-١٧-٠٤-٠٢, First Edition – Amendment 1) issued by SASO governs new automotive spare parts sold in Saudi Arabia, whether manufactured domestically or imported. It covers spare parts under 11 HS codes (4010, 4016, 7007, 7009, 8301, 8302, 8421, 8511, 8512, 8544, 8708), excluding tires, car batteries, and safety barriers (covered under separate regulations). Suppliers must ensure parts comply with 67 referenced standards, maintain quality management systems (ISO/TS 16949 or equivalent), and obtain conformity certificates from SASO-accepted bodies via Type 3 assessment per ISO/IEC 17067. Vehicle manufacturers and their legal representatives may self-declare conformity via SDoC. Packaging must meet specific labeling requirements in Arabic. Market surveillance authorities and regulatory bodies may conduct random sampling and withdraw non-compliant products. Violations are subject to penalties under the Commercial Fraud Prevention Law.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Machinery Safety, Part Two: Mobile Machinery and Heavy Equipment
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لسلامة الآلات، الجزء الثاني: الآلات المتنقلة ومعدات الأشغال الثقيلة
summary:
This is the Technical Regulation for Machinery Safety, Part Two: Mobile Machinery and Heavy Equipment, issued by SASO (Board Meeting No. 180, dated 08/26/1442H / 04/08/2021) and published in the Official Gazette on 10/09/1442H. It covers mobile machinery, heavy construction equipment, safety components and interchangeable equipment including earth-moving machinery, agricultural tractors and machinery, rock drilling equipment, road construction machinery, and associated batteries (HS codes 8424, 8429, 8430, 8431, 8432, 8433, 8479). Suppliers must meet essential health and safety requirements through risk analysis, appropriate design, protective measures, and informational safeguards as detailed in Annex 2. Technical requirements are specified in 139 referenced standards listed in Annex 1-أ. Conformity is demonstrated through Type 1a assessment per ISO/IEC 17067, requiring a Type Approval Certificate from an SASO-accepted body plus a Supplier Declaration of Conformity. Used machinery requires an inspection certificate. Safety components and spare parts from manufacturers or their official representatives for certified models are exempt from conformity assessment. Violations are subject to penalties under the Commercial Fraud Prevention Law. Suppliers had 180 days from publication to comply and 365 days to correct market status.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Doors, Windows and their Accessories
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للأبواب والنوافذ وملحقاتها
summary:
This technical regulation issued by SASO (Board Decision 177, approved October 2020, amended June 2023) establishes mandatory requirements for doors, windows, and their accessories used in buildings in Saudi Arabia. It covers products across HS codes 3925, 4418, 6914, 7308, 7508, 7602, 7610, 8301, and 8302. Basic requirements address fitness for purpose, sustainable resource use, fire safety, hygiene and environmental health, safety in use, and energy efficiency/thermal insulation. Two conformity assessment procedures are required: Type 3 (ISO/IEC 17067) with production process management system certification by an accepted body for fire-resistant products, and Type 1a (ISO/IEC 17067) type approval for all other products. Suppliers must obtain conformity certificates from SASO-accepted bodies, issue supplier declarations of conformity, and maintain technical documentation for 10 years. Products with Saudi Quality Mark are deemed compliant. Non-compliance may result in product withdrawal, recall, certificate cancellation, and penalties under the Anti-Commercial Fraud Law.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Gas Appliances and Their Accessories
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للأجهزة التي تعمل بالغاز وملحقاتها
summary:
This technical regulation (issued by SASO, approved at Board Meeting No. 156, published in Official Gazette with Amendment 3 on 16/02/2024) establishes mandatory safety and performance requirements for LPG-fuelled gas appliances and their accessories intended for cooking, heating, water heating, cooling and lighting, as well as accessories including safety equipment, monitoring and control devices, and small directly-connected LPG cylinders. Industrial-use appliances are excluded. Suppliers must obtain the Saudi Quality Mark through an SASO-accepted conformity assessment body, prepare a technical file containing a Supplier Declaration of Conformity and risk assessment, and ensure compliance with 25 referenced Saudi/international standards covering domestic cookers, heaters, water heaters, pressure regulators, gas cylinders, hoses, valves, and catering equipment. The regulation covers HS code categories including gas containers, cooking appliances, water heaters, valves, and pressure regulators. Non-compliance may result in product withdrawal, recall, export, destruction, certificate cancellation, and penalties under the Commercial Fraud Prevention Law.

---

name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للأجهزة الكهربائية والمعدات المعدّة للاستخدام في الأجواء القابلة للانفجار
summary:
This is the Saudi technical regulation for electrical equipment and devices intended for use in potentially explosive atmospheres, issued by SASO (Board Decision No. 194, dated 13/03/1445H / 28/09/2023), published in the Official Gazette on 03/05/1445H (17/11/2023M). The regulation covers equipment, protective systems, safety devices, control/regulation devices used outside explosive atmospheres, and components intended for incorporation into such equipment. It excludes medical devices, military equipment, personal protective equipment (covered by a separate regulation), maritime vessels, transport vehicles (except those designed for explosive atmospheres), and domestic appliances with rare gas leak risk. Equipment is classified into Group I (mining) with categories M1 and M2, and Group II (other hazardous locations) with categories 1, 2, and 3, each requiring different levels of explosion protection. Suppliers must obtain a SASO conformity certificate, prepare a Supplier Declaration of Conformity, and provide a risk assessment document. The regulation references 116 standards covering Ex equipment, explosion prevention, detection equipment, and related applications. Compliance enforcement is carried out by regulatory authorities and market surveillance authorities with powers to withdraw, recall, and penalize non-compliant products. Transitional provisions allow 365 days for implementation and 548 days for suppliers to correct market status.

---

name_ar: اللائحة الفنية الخليجية للأجهزة والمعدات الكهربائية منخفضة الجهد
summary:
This is the second edition of the Gulf Technical Regulation for Low Voltage Electrical Equipment and Appliances (BD-142004-01), replacing the first edition (BD07070503) dated 2007. It applies to all electrical and electronic equipment designed for use at rated voltages between 50-1000V AC or 75-1500V DC, with specific exclusions listed in Annex 2 (e.g., explosion-proof equipment, medical radiation equipment, elevator electrical parts, electricity meters, electric fence controllers, radio interference equipment, and specialized equipment for ships/aircraft/railways). The regulation establishes mandatory basic safety requirements covering protection from electrical and non-electrical hazards, electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) requirements, country-specific voltage/frequency/plug standards for all 7 GCC member states plus Yemen, and clearly defines obligations for all economic operators (manufacturers, authorized representatives, importers, distributors). Conformity assessment follows a two-tier risk-based approach: List 1 products require manufacturer self-declaration with technical documentation; List 2 products require mandatory third-party type examination by a Notified Body with a Type Examination Certificate reviewed at least every 3 years. IECEE CB Scheme certification is accepted as an equivalent conformity assessment path. All products must bear the Gulf conformity mark and be accompanied by Arabic-language safety instructions. Declarations of conformity and technical documentation must be retained for 10 years. The regulation is effective from 1 July 2016.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Footwear and Accessories
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للأحذية وملحقاتها
summary:
This technical regulation issued by SASO (approved at Board Meeting No. 177, dated 28/02/1442H, published in Official Gazette on 12/04/1442H) establishes mandatory safety, quality, labelling, and conformity assessment requirements for all types of footwear (leather, textile, plastic, or mixed materials) placed on the Saudi market, covering HS codes 6401–6406 and certain classified items. Excluded are medical footwear and footwear classified as children's toys. Professional/safety footwear is additionally subject to the PPE Technical Regulation. Footwear made from or incorporating religiously prohibited leathers is banned. Suppliers must obtain a conformity certificate from an accepted CAB: Type 3 (production process assessment) for infant footwear (under 36 months), and Type 1a (Type Approval) for all other footwear. A Supplier Declaration of Conformity must accompany each approved product type. Technical documentation must be retained for 10 years. Non-compliance is subject to penalties under the Anti-Commercial Fraud Law and consumer protection legislation. A 6-month transition period applies for compliance, with a maximum 1-year period for trading of non-compliant existing stock.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Electrical Batteries
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للبطاريات الكهربائية
summary:
This SASO Technical Regulation for Electrical Batteries (Regulation ID: م.إ-١٦٦-١٨-٠٤-٠٢, First Edition Amendment 1, published December 2018, amended November 2022) establishes mandatory health, safety, labeling, and conformity assessment requirements for all electrical batteries sold in Saudi Arabia under HS codes 8506 (primary cells and batteries) and 8507 (primary and secondary cells and batteries). The regulation covers automotive batteries and portable batteries (button cells ≤1kg), excluding batteries used in military, medical equipment, and space applications. Suppliers must meet technical requirements on product identification, battery classification, electrical characteristics (polarity marking), mechanical properties (vibration resistance, packaging), and chemical restrictions (mercury ≤0.0005% and cadmium ≤0.002% by weight, with limited exceptions). Conformity assessment follows Type 3 per ISO/IEC 17067, requiring suppliers to obtain a conformity certificate from a SASO-accepted third-party body through evaluation and approval of a Product Safety Management System (PSMS), with periodic surveillance every 3 years. Suppliers must also issue a Supplier Declaration of Conformity and maintain technical documentation for 10 years. The regulation references 71 Saudi/international standards across battery types. Non-compliance results in product withdrawal, recall, certificate cancellation, and penalties under the Commercial Fraud Law.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Electromagnetic Compatibility
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للتوافق الكهرومغناطيسي
summary:
This regulation establishes mandatory electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) requirements for electrical and electronic equipment (apparatus and fixed installations) placed on the Saudi market. It covers a broad range of products including household appliances, industrial equipment, lighting, switchgear, metering devices, vehicles, and measurement instruments, as defined by 46 HS code headings. Excluded are radio/telecom terminal equipment, aviation equipment, amateur radio equipment, medical devices, equipment incapable of generating/being affected by EMC disturbance, and electronic evaluation boards for R&D. Suppliers must ensure equipment does not exceed emission limits affecting other devices and has adequate immunity. Conformity is demonstrated through supplier self-declaration (SDoC) supported by an internal production control technical file, without mandatory third-party certification. The regulation was approved on 28 September 2023 and published on 17 November 2023, with mandatory application within 180 days and a 365-day market correction period.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Restriction of Hazardous Substances in Electrical and Electronic Equipment
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للحد من المواد الخطرة في الأجهزة والمعدات الكهربائية والإلكترونية
summary:
This Saudi technical regulation (Second Edition, approved June 2022) establishes mandatory restrictions on hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment (EEE) and their spare parts placed on the Saudi market, whether locally manufactured or imported. It covers six product categories: household appliances, ICT equipment, lighting equipment, electrical/electronic tools and equipment, toys/leisure/sports equipment, and monitoring and control instruments. The regulation sets maximum concentration limits for six hazardous substances (Lead, Mercury, Cadmium, Hexavalent Chromium, PBB, PBDE) in homogeneous materials, with specific exemptions detailed in Annexes 1-أ and 1-ب. Conformity assessment requires a Type 1a certificate (per ISO/IEC 17067) from an SASO-accepted body, or a Supplier Declaration of Conformity when the manufacturer or legal representative supplies directly. Products must be accompanied by a technical file including risk assessment and operating instructions. The regulation references 13 SASO/IEC standards for testing and documentation. Non-compliance may result in product withdrawal, recall, certificate cancellation, and penalties under Saudi commercial fraud laws.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Escalators and Moving Walks
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للسلالم والممرات المتحركة
summary:
This technical regulation establishes mandatory safety and performance requirements for escalators and moving walks and their accessories in Saudi Arabia, approved by SASO Board Meeting No. 184 (31/03/2022) and published in the Official Gazette on 20/05/2022. The regulation covers mechanical, electrical, safety, control, and braking systems and references 11 Saudi standards based on EN 115, ISO 14798, ISO 9589, ISO 25740, ISO 25745, ISO 22199, ISO 22200, EN 13015, and ISO 13850. Conformity assessment requires Type 1a (Type Approval) from an SASO-accepted body, plus an inspection certificate from an ISO 17020-accredited inspection body before commissioning, and annual periodic inspections. Suppliers must maintain a technical file and provide a Supplier Declaration of Conformity. Obligations are defined separately for suppliers, operating entities, maintenance entities, and inspection bodies. Products bearing the Saudi Quality Mark or equivalent are deemed compliant. Transitional period: regulations apply within 180 days of publication, with a 365-day grace period for suppliers to rectify market compliance.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Tanks - Part 3: Dry Gas Transport Tanks
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للصهاريج – الجزء ٣: صهاريج نقل الغاز الجاف
summary:
This regulation establishes mandatory requirements for dry gas transport tanks (both compressed natural gas CNG and liquefied natural gas LNG) in Saudi Arabia. It covers design, manufacturing, inspection, and testing requirements referencing 15 international and regional standards. Suppliers must obtain conformity certificates from SASO-accepted third-party bodies per Type 1a of ISO/IEC 17067, and tanks must undergo pre-service and annual periodic inspections. Manufacturers must hold ISO 9001 quality management certification and be registered with SASO. The regulation applies to freight vehicles (HS 8704) and tank trailers/semi-trailers (HS 8716). A 180-day implementation period applies from official gazette publication, with a 365-day grace period for tanks already in service.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Tankers – Part 2: LPG Transport Tankers
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للصهاريج – الجزء الثاني: صهاريج نقل الغاز البترولي المسال
summary:
This technical regulation issued by SASO (approved January 2022, published March 2022) establishes mandatory requirements for LPG (Liquefied Petroleum Gas) transport tankers in Saudi Arabia, covering trucks (HS 8704) and trailers/semi-trailers (HS 8716). It specifies design, manufacturing, inspection, and testing requirements per seven referenced SASO/EN standards. Conformity assessment follows Type 1a (Type Approval) per ISO/IEC 17067, conducted by SASO-accepted bodies. Tankers must undergo pre-service inspection and annual periodic inspections. Manufacturers must be registered with SASO, maintain an effective quality management system, and issue conformity certificates. Non-compliant products are subject to market withdrawal, recall, and penalties under Saudi commercial fraud regulations.

---

name_ar: اللائحة الفنية الخليجية للعب الأطفال
summary:
This is the second edition (BD131704-01) of the Gulf Technical Regulation for Children's Toys, replacing the first edition BD07070502 dated 27/11/2007, effective from 1 January 2014. It applies to all products designed or intended for play by children under 14 years of age, sold in GCC member states. The regulation establishes mandatory essential safety requirements covering physical/mechanical properties, flammability, chemical properties (including CMR substance prohibitions, element migration limits, allergenic fragrance restrictions, phthalate restrictions, and nitrosamine prohibitions), electrical properties, hygiene, and radioactivity. Economic operators (manufacturers, authorized representatives, importers, distributors) have defined responsibilities. Conformity assessment follows either a manufacturer self-declaration path (with optional Type Examination by an Accepted Body) or an importer third-party verification path (individual or statistical testing by an Accepted Body). Products must bear the Gulf Conformity Mark. Annex 8 contains an extensive list of CMR substances prohibited or restricted in toys. The regulation references GSO 1943 for cosmetic toy kits.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Adhesives and Glues
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للغراء والمواد اللاصقة
summary:
This technical regulation covers all adhesive products and glues in the form of tapes, liquids, powders, or pastes placed on the Saudi market. It excludes construction adhesives (covered by building materials regulations) and medical/food adhesives (regulated by SFDA). The regulation establishes basic safety, environmental, metrological, and packaging requirements. Suppliers must ensure products comply with the referenced Saudi standards listed in Annex 1. Conformity assessment is mandatory: Type 3 (production process assessment with certified product safety management system) for liquid and hot-melt adhesives; Type 1a (Type Approval) for all other covered products. Both procedures require certification by a SASO-accepted conformity assessment body. A Supplier Declaration of Conformity must accompany all products. Labelling must be in Arabic. Non-compliant products are subject to withdrawal, recall, and penalties under the anti-commercial fraud law. The regulation entered into force 180 days after publication in the Official Gazette (published 17/12/2021), with a 365-day correction period for existing market products.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for General Safety Requirements of Machinery
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمتطلبات العامة لسلامة الآلات
summary:
This regulation establishes mandatory general safety requirements for machinery sold in Saudi Arabia, covering: (a) Machinery, (b) Interchangeable equipment, (c) Safety components, (d) Lifting accessories, (e) Chains, ropes and straps, (f) Removable mechanical transmission devices, and (g) Partly completed machinery. It applies to a broad range of industrial machinery under HS chapters 8419-8515 and 9022. Suppliers must conduct risk assessments, design machinery to meet essential health and safety requirements (detailed in Annex 2), obtain Type 1a conformity assessment from SASO-accepted bodies, and maintain technical documentation. The regulation references over 569 Saudi/GSO/ISO/IEC/EN standards. Excluded products include amusement park equipment, nuclear machinery, certain transport vehicles, medical devices, and machinery covered under other specific SASO technical regulations. Compliance period is 180 days from official gazette publication with a 365-day grace period for market correction.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Watercrafts
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمراكب المائية
summary:
This SASO Technical Regulation for Watercrafts (approved April 2023, published May 2023) establishes mandatory safety, environmental and conformity requirements for recreational watercraft (hull length 2.5-24m), personal watercraft (jet skis), watercraft components (ignition protection equipment, steering systems, fuel tanks/hoses, hatches, navigation lights), and propulsion engines placed on the Saudi market. It covers design and construction requirements (design categories A-D, identification, stability, buoyancy, structural integrity, fire protection, electrical systems, fuel systems, gas systems, waste discharge), exhaust emission limits for propulsion engines (CO, HC, NOx, particulates by engine type and power), and noise emission limits. Two conformity assessment models apply: Type 1a (type approval by accepted body) for most product categories, and Type 3 (production process quality system assessment with periodic surveillance) for larger design A/B craft (12-24m). All products require a certificate of conformity from an accepted body, a supplier declaration of conformity, risk assessment, and owner's manual in Arabic. Vessels with Saudi Quality Mark are deemed compliant. Enforcement is through regulatory authorities at customs and market surveillance authorities in the market.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Electric Vehicles
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمركبات الكهربائية
summary:
This is Version 3 of the Saudi SASO Technical Regulation for Electric Vehicles (م.إ-١٦٢-١٧-٠٤-٠٣), approved by the SASO Board of Directors on 03/10/2024 and published in the Official Gazette on 03/01/2025. It applies to Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) and Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicles (PHEV) of categories M (passenger) and N (freight) with design speeds exceeding 25 km/h. The regulation sets mandatory safety, performance, and conformity assessment requirements. Key requirements include electrical shock protection, REESS safety, crash resistance, minimum 200 km electric range, and mandatory use of Type 2 (AC) or CCS2 (DC) charging connectors only. Suppliers must obtain a Type 1a Certificate of Conformity from SASO or an SASO-accepted CAB, maintain a technical file, and provide a Supplier Declaration of Conformity. The regulation references over 200 Saudi, GCC, international (IEC, ISO, UNECE, SAE, FMVSS, CISPR) standards. Implementation period is 180 days from publication with a 365-day transition period for market compliance.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Used Imported Vehicles
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمركبات المستوردة المستعملة
summary:
This technical regulation governs the importation of used motor vehicles (light and heavy) into Saudi Arabia under HS heading 8700. It establishes mandatory basic requirements and conformity assessment procedures that importers must fulfill before supplying vehicles to the Saudi market. Key requirements include compliance with Saudi standards for vehicle safety, dimensions, VIN integrity, fuel economy, tyres, brakes, lighting, and emissions. Prohibited vehicles include salvage, taxi/police, school transport, right-hand drive, armored (non-military), and gas-to-petrol/diesel converted vehicles. Electric vehicles are excluded. Conformity is assessed through physical inspection by an SASO-accepted inspection body using a standardized checklist (Annex 2), resulting in a certificate of inspection. Additional modern safety systems (ABS, ESC, OBD, airbags, TPMS, BOS) are mandatory for model year 2018 and above. The regulation was approved in January 2021 and amended with Amendment 1 published in June 2023.

---

name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمركبات المعدلة والطرازات ذات الإنتاج المحدود
summary:
This technical regulation (م.إ-١٦٦-١٨-٠٤-٠٣), approved by SASO Board Resolution 166 on 13 September 2018, establishes mandatory requirements for: (1) modified light vehicles (new or used, GVW ≤ 3500 kg), (2) limited production model vehicles, and (3) vehicles under exceptional conditions. It covers technical requirements for modified components (engine, transmission, exhaust, fuel system, brakes, steering, tyres, entertainment systems, chassis), emissions and noise compliance, and conformity assessment. Conformity must be demonstrated through inspection by an SASO-accepted ISO 17020-accredited inspection body, supplemented by a supplier declaration of conformity and a complete technical file. Motorcycles, trailers, semi-trailers, mobile homes, and military/security vehicles are excluded. No HS code annex is included in this regulation.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Hydrogen Vehicles
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمركبات الهيدروجينية
summary:
The Technical Regulation for Hydrogen Vehicles (issued by SASO, approved January 2022, published March 2022) establishes mandatory safety and performance requirements for hydrogen-powered vehicles (categories M and N) and their hydrogen components and systems sold in Saudi Arabia. The regulation covers vehicles using hydrogen as fuel, their hydrogen components (tanks, valves, regulators, heat exchangers, hoses, sensors), and hydrogen systems including new forms of hydrogen storage or use. Suppliers (manufacturers/importers) must obtain Type Approval (اعتماد الطراز) from an SASO-accepted conformity assessment body following a Type 1a procedure per ISO/IEC 17067, submit a Supplier Declaration of Conformity, and maintain a technical file with test reports, risk assessment, and applicable standards list. Vehicles must also comply with the Technical Regulation for Electric Vehicles. The regulation references 79 standards covering hydrogen systems, vehicle safety, tyres, braking, glazing, and electrical systems. The HS code annex lists only code 8702 (vehicles for 10+ persons), though the regulation scope explicitly covers M and N category vehicles. Transitional period: 180 days for application from official gazette publication, 365 days for suppliers to correct market status.

---

name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمركبات ذاتية القيادة
summary:
This regulation establishes mandatory safety requirements for fully autonomous vehicles (SAE L4/L5) of vehicle categories M (passenger) and N (goods) operating in Saudi Arabia, whether locally manufactured or imported. It covers three use cases: point-to-point autonomous vehicles, hub-to-hub autonomous vehicles, and automated valet parking. The regulation requires compliance with an extensive set of basic requirements covering traffic scenario handling, ADS safety concept validation, Safety Management System audits, cybersecurity (UN-R 155), software update management (UN-R 156), minimum risk maneuvers, human-machine interaction, and event data recording. Conformity assessment follows Type 1a model requiring manufacturer self-declaration plus type approval by an SASO-accepted body. The type approval body must review technical documentation, audit the SMS, and witness/conduct physical and road tests. Annex 3 provides specific exceptions and adaptations to traditional vehicle standards (such as crash tests, braking, steering, EMC) for the different sub-types of autonomous vehicles. Annual in-service safety reports are mandatory. The regulation applies within 180 days of official gazette publication.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Electric Lifts Used in Buildings and Facilities
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمصاعد الكهربائية المستخدمة في المباني والمنشآت
summary:
This technical regulation governs electric lifts used in buildings and facilities in Saudi Arabia (HS 84281000), including their safety components. It establishes mandatory health and safety requirements covering car design, shaft construction, suspension means, machinery, control systems, doors, and electrical safety. Suppliers must obtain a conformity certificate via Type 3 assessment per ISO/IEC 17067 (type approval + product safety management system) from a SASO-accepted CAB. Installed lifts must be inspected by a SASO-accepted inspection body (accredited per ISO/IEC 17020) before commissioning and annually thereafter, registered with the competent authority (Municipalities/Civil Defense), and maintained under a maintenance contract. Buildings over 23m must have at least one firefighter lift. The regulation assigns obligations to suppliers, inspection bodies, maintenance companies, owners, competent authorities, regulatory bodies, and market surveillance authorities. Non-compliance may result in product withdrawal, fines under commercial fraud laws, and removal from service.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Degradable Plastic Products
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمنتجات البلاستيكية القابلة للتحلل
summary:
This SASO technical regulation, published on 14/10/2016, establishes mandatory environmental requirements for polyethylene and polypropylene plastic products intended for short-term use (e.g., carrier bags, packaging films, disposable personal care products, agricultural films) as listed in Annex 1. All covered products must be OXO-biodegradable (degradable by oxidation then biologically) in accordance with the standards listed in Annex 2, primarily SASO 2879. Products must obtain a SASO logo usage license (valid 1 year, renewable) through a Type 1a conformity assessment procedure per ISO/IEC 17067. The manufacturer must hold an ISO 14001 environmental management certificate, submit test reports from an ISO/IEC 17025-accredited registered laboratory, and provide a supplier declaration of conformity. The logo must be affixed on all licensed products. Medical and food industry plastics are excluded. Market surveillance authorities and regulatory bodies are empowered to conduct random sampling and testing, and to withdraw non-compliant products. Penalties follow the Commercial Fraud Law.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Textile Products
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمنتجات النسيجية
summary:
This is the Second Edition of the SASO Technical Regulation for Textile Products (Regulation ID: م.أ-١٦٤-١٨-٠٥-٠٢), approved by SASO Board of Directors in Meeting No. 195 on 15/06/1445H (28/12/2023) and published in the Official Gazette on 30/06/1445H (12/01/2024). It applies to all yarn and textile products offered in Saudi Arabian markets containing at least 80% by weight of textile fibers, covering 14 HS code ranges from chapters 50-63. Key requirements include mandatory Arabic-language labeling with fiber composition (names and percentages in descending weight order), country of origin, supplier details, care/washing symbols, and recycling instructions. Safety requirements address pH levels, color fastness, hazardous chemicals (formaldehyde, heavy metals, carcinogenic amines, phthalates, etc.), accessories safety (buttons, cords in children's clothing), fire retardancy, and filling material contamination. Two conformity assessment tracks apply: Type 3 per ISO/IEC 17067 (production quality assurance with CAB surveillance) for children's textiles under 36 months and underwear/innerwear; Type 1a per ISO/IEC 17067 (type approval) for all other textile products. Suppliers must maintain technical files and conformity documents for 10 years. Manufacturers must have an effective environmental management system (ISO 14001 accepted). The regulation excludes medical-purpose products (under SFDA) and footwear (under footwear regulation). Transitional period: regulation applies within 180 days of Official Gazette publication, with a 365-day grace period for suppliers to correct market compliance.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Detergents
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمنظفات
summary:
This technical regulation (م.أ-١٥٥-١٦-٠٣-٠٢), first issued in 2016 and amended in 2024, establishes mandatory requirements for detergents, surfactants, and soaps placed on the Saudi market. It covers products under HS headings 2828, 2836, 32.04, 34.01, 34.02, and 38.09, excluding air fresheners. Suppliers must ensure products comply with 27 referenced Saudi/GSO standards, maintain complete MSDS documentation with ingredient weight breakdowns, and meet labeling, packaging, and metrological requirements. Conformity assessment follows Type 3 per ISO/IEC 17067, requiring certification by a SASO-accepted body through product safety management system evaluation and periodic factory surveillance. Suppliers must also issue a Declaration of Conformity and maintain technical files for at least 10 years. Non-compliant products are subject to withdrawal, recall, and penalties under the Commercial Fraud Law.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Paper and Cardboard
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للورق والكرتون
summary:
This Saudi technical regulation (First Edition, Amendment 1) establishes mandatory requirements for paper and cardboard products placed on the Saudi market, including graphic paper (printing, writing, newsprint), packaging paper and cardboard, kraft paper, paperboard, tissue/sanitary paper, industrial paper, and paper pulp. Excluded are food-contact paper products, wallpaper, wet wipes, perfumed tissues, and cosmetic wipes governed by other regulations. The regulation covers technical, environmental, chemical safety, metrology, and packaging requirements. It mandates two different conformity assessment procedures: Type 3 (per ISO/IEC 17067) based on product safety management system evaluation for sanitary paper products, and Type 1A (Type Approval per ISO/IEC 17067) for all other covered products. Both require certification from a SASO-accepted conformity assessment body. A comprehensive technical file including supplier declaration, risk assessment, origin certificate, test reports, and applicable standards list must accompany products. Products with the Saudi Quality Mark are deemed compliant. Market surveillance authorities and regulatory bodies have authority to sample, test, and withdraw non-compliant products.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Standby and Off Mode Requirements in Electrical and Electronic Equipment
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمتطلبات وضع الاستعداد والسكون في الأجهزة الكهربائية والإلكترونية
summary:
This SASO technical regulation establishes mandatory standby and off-mode power consumption requirements for electrical and electronic equipment sold in Saudi Arabia, including clothes dryers (HS 8421), dishwashers (HS 8422), washing machines (HS 8450), microwave/electric ovens (HS 8516), and televisions/electronic displays (HS 8528). Effective from 01/07/2024. The regulation sets maximum power limits for off-mode (0.5W), standby (0.5W–1W), and networked standby (2W) modes, and mandates power management functionality enabled by default. Conformity assessment is based on supplier self-declaration with internal production control (Type A), referencing EN 50564 and EN 50643. Suppliers must prepare a technical file with test reports and retain documentation for 10 years. Regulatory authorities may conduct random sampling for market surveillance.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Pressure Equipment
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمعدات الضغط
summary:
This Saudi Technical Regulation for Pressure Equipment (issued by SASO Board Resolution No. 187, dated 06/10/2022, published 16/12/2022) establishes mandatory safety and conformity assessment requirements for pressure equipment with maximum allowable pressure (PS) greater than 0.5 bar. Covered products include pressure vessels, piping, safety accessories, pressure accessories, and assemblies (HS codes: 7311, 7419, 7613, 8402, 8403, 8404, 8414, 8468, 8481). Equipment is classified into four hazard categories (I-IV) based on pressure, volume/nominal diameter, and fluid group (hazardous vs. non-hazardous). Categories I-II require Type 1a conformity assessment (type approval) from an accepted body; Categories III-IV require Type 3 (type approval plus production quality system assessment with periodic surveillance). Category I products supplied by the manufacturer/legal representative may use Supplier Declaration of Conformity only. Essential safety requirements in Annex 2 cover design for adequate strength, material selection, manufacturing procedures, permanent joints, non-destructive testing, final inspection including hydrostatic testing, safety accessories, and operating instructions. The regulation references 194 standards (Annex 1-A) covering valves, boilers, pressure vessels, piping, materials, and flanges. Labeling must be in Arabic and include manufacturer identification, year of manufacture, and permissible limits. The regulation entered into force within 180 days of publication, with a 365-day transition period for market correction.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Special-Use Vehicle Equipment and Accessories
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمعدات وتجهيزات المركبات ذات الاستخدام الخاص
summary:
This technical regulation governs equipment and accessories for special-use vehicles in Saudi Arabia (HS code 8705), primarily covering towing and transport trucks (car carriers/السطحات). It mandates compliance with SASO 2971, conformity certification via Type 1-a (ISO/IEC 17067) from SASO or an authorized body, periodic inspections every 3 years, and a Supplier Declaration of Conformity. Towing trucks with Gulf conformity certificates from model year 2025 are deemed compliant. The regulation was approved on 27/06/2024 and published in the Official Gazette on 23/08/2024, with an 180-day implementation period and a 365-day grace period for existing in-service equipment.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Personal Protective Equipment and Clothing
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمعدات وملابس الوقاية الشخصية
summary:
اللائحة الفنية لمعدات وملابس الوقاية الشخصية (م.إ-١٦٥-١٨-٠٤-٠٤) صادرة عن الهيئة السعودية للمواصفات والمقاييس والجودة، اعتُمدت عام 2018م مع تعديل ثانٍ نُشر في 2023م. تغطي اللائحة طيفاً واسعاً من معدات وملابس الوقاية الشخصية المُعدّة لحماية صحة وسلامة المستخدمين من المخاطر المحتملة، وتستثني المعدات الطبية والعسكرية. تشترط اللائحة مطابقة المنتجات للمتطلبات الأساسية للصحة والسلامة الواردة في الملحق (٢) واستيفاء متطلبات 178 مواصفة قياسية دولية ومحلية. يجب على الموردين الحصول على شهادة مطابقة من جهة مقبولة وفقاً لنموذج تقويم المطابقة (Type 3) وفق ISO/IEC 17067، القائم على تقويم عملية الإنتاج ونظام إدارة سلامة المنتج، مع مراقبة دورية لمدة ثلاث سنوات. تُطبق اللائحة خلال 180 يوماً من النشر مع مهلة تصحيح 365 يوماً للموردين.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Building Materials Part 1: Metal Sections and Their Alloys for Construction and Buildings
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمواد البناء الجزء الأول: قطاعات المعادن وسبائكها للإنشاءات والمباني
summary:
This technical regulation (م.إ-١٥٦-١٦-٠٤-٠١, Second Edition Amendment 1, published 2022) covers metal sections and their alloys designed for construction and building works in Saudi Arabia, including carbon steel bars, structural steel shapes, stainless steel, hollow sections, aluminum and aluminum alloy products, and related products across 34 HS code categories (7208-9406). Suppliers must ensure products meet basic requirements for mechanical resistance, fire safety, and sustainable resource use as specified in the referenced SASO/ASTM and SASO/ISO standards listed in Annex 1. Conformity assessment follows two paths: (1) reinforcement steel and flat steel products require the Saudi Quality Mark; (2) all other products require Type 3 conformity assessment per ISO/IEC 17067 through an accepted body, including Product Safety Management System certification, factory audits, and periodic surveillance. Products must comply with Saudi Building Code requirements in addition to this regulation. Violations are subject to penalties under the commercial fraud regulations.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Building Materials - Part Five: Pipes Used in Water, Electricity, and Gas Networks
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمواد البناء الجزء الخامس: الأنابيب المستخدمة في شبكات تمديدات المياه والكهرباء والغاز
summary:
This regulation (م.إ-171-19-06-02) establishes mandatory requirements for pipes used in water (hot, cold, sewage), electricity, and gas network installations in buildings and structures in Saudi Arabia, including pipe fittings and accessories. It covers plastic pipes (PE, PP, PVC, CPVC, PEX, etc.), metallic pipes (ductile iron, cast iron, stainless steel, galvanized steel), copper pipes, aluminum pipes, and electrical conduit pipes. Excluded are fire suppression water pipes. Pipes must meet basic requirements for structural suitability, fire safety, health and environment, safety in use, and energy efficiency. Conformity assessment follows Type 3 per ISO/IEC 17067, requiring certification of a Product Safety Management System by a SASO-accepted body with 3-year periodic surveillance. A Supplier Declaration of Conformity must accompany products. Products with the Saudi Quality Mark are deemed compliant. The regulation references 106 technical standards (SASO-ISO, SASO-ASTM, SASO-IEC, SASO-EN series). Non-compliance is subject to penalties under the Commercial Fraud Law.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Building Materials - Part Four: Bricks, Tiles, Ceramics, Sanitary Ware and Related Products
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمواد البناء - الجزء الرابع: الطوب والبلاط والسيراميك والأدوات الصحية والمنتجات ذات العلاقة
summary:
This regulation (Regulation ID: م.إ-١٦١-١٧-٠٧-٠٣, Second Edition - Amendment 1) establishes mandatory technical requirements for building materials including ceramic tiles, clay tiles (roofing/pitched), natural and artificial stone, marble, sanitary ware (ceramic and non-ceramic), and related products (bricks, flooring tiles of various compositions) intended for use in construction and buildings. It excludes cement tiles and cement bricks. Products must meet seven basic requirements: mechanical resistance, fire safety, sustainable resource use, health/safety/environment, safety in use, noise protection, and energy efficiency. Ceramic tiles require the Saudi Quality Mark or equivalent; all other covered products require Type 3 conformity assessment (per ISO/IEC 17067) by an accepted third-party body, involving product safety management system evaluation and periodic surveillance. Suppliers must maintain a technical file including a Supplier Declaration of Conformity, risk assessment, and country of origin documentation for at least 10 years. The regulation was approved in 2018 and published in 2019, with Amendment 1 published in January 2022, and applies within 180 days of publication in the official gazette.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Building Materials - Part Three: Hydraulic Binders and Related Products
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمواد البناء الجزء الثالث: الروابط الهيدروليكية والمنتجات ذات العلاقة
summary:
This regulation establishes mandatory technical requirements for hydraulic binders and related building materials including cement, lime, gypsum, ready-mixed concrete, mortars, masonry units, aggregates, and chemical admixtures intended for use in construction. Products must meet fundamental requirements for mechanical resistance, fire safety, sustainable resource use, health/safety/environment protection, safe use, noise protection, and energy efficiency. For cement and ready-mixed concrete, suppliers must obtain the Saudi Quality Mark (علامة الجودة السعودية) through a Type 5 certification scheme per ISO/IEC TR 17026. For all other covered products, a Type 3 conformity assessment certificate from an accepted third-party body per ISO/IEC 17067 is required. Suppliers must maintain a technical file including a Supplier Declaration of Conformity, risk assessment, and certificate of origin. Products must bear conformity marks, and documentation must be retained for 10 years. The regulation was approved in 2017 and amended in 2022.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Building Materials - Part 2: Insulation Materials and Cladding Materials for Buildings
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمواد البناء - الجزء الثاني: مواد العزل ومواد التكسية للمباني
summary:
This Technical Regulation (م.إ-١٥٦-١٦-٠٤-٠٢), issued by SASO (Second Edition, Amendment 1, published 14 January 2022), establishes mandatory requirements for insulation materials and cladding materials used in buildings in Saudi Arabia. It covers a broad range of products including thermal, acoustic and waterproofing insulation, and building cladding systems, excluding tiles, ceramics, marble, natural/artificial stone and paints. Suppliers must ensure products meet essential requirements for fire safety, hygiene/health/environment, noise protection, energy efficiency, and sustainable resource use, and comply with the referenced technical standards listed in Annex 1. Conformity assessment follows two tracks: cladding materials require the Saudi Quality Mark, while all other products require Type 3 certification per ISO/IEC 17067 from an SASO-accepted CAB. Products with Saudi Quality Mark or equivalent are deemed compliant. A technical file including a Declaration of Conformity and risk assessment document must accompany all products. Regulation also establishes obligations for regulatory authorities at ports/factories and market surveillance authorities, including powers to withdraw non-conforming products and impose penalties under the Anti-Commercial Fraud Law.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Leather Products
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمنتجات الجلدية
summary:
The Saudi Technical Regulation for Leather Products (م.إ-١٧٩-٢١-٠٩-٠٣), approved January 2021 and published July 2021, establishes mandatory safety, chemical, labeling, and conformity assessment requirements for all natural and synthetic leather products and leather goods sold in Saudi Arabia, excluding footwear and raw pre-tanned animal hides. Key requirements include limits on hazardous substances (Chromium VI, aromatic amines, formaldehyde, chlorinated phenols, harmful dyes), prohibition on endangered or Islamically-forbidden animal hides, environmental management system certification (ISO 14001), and Arabic-language labeling. Children's leather products (under 36 months) require Type 3 conformity assessment (production process surveillance by accepted body), while other products require Type 1a (type approval). Both require a Supplier Declaration of Conformity. The regulation covers 18 HS code categories from chapters 41, 42, 43, 65, and 91.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Food Safety in Kitchen Tools and Appliances
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للسلامة الغذائية في الأدوات والأجهزة المستخدمة في المطبخ
summary:
This is the Technical Regulation for Food Safety in Kitchen Tools and Appliances (م.إ-١٧١-١٩-٠٦-٠٤), issued by SASO (Board Meeting 171, June 2019) with Amendment 1 published January 2021. It covers all kitchen tools and appliances that directly or potentially contact food during preparation, serving, and storage, as listed in Annex 2. The regulation requires that food-contact materials do not transfer harmful components to food, comply with the referenced Saudi and international standards in Annex 2-أ, and meet food safety, packaging, labeling, and traceability requirements. Suppliers must obtain a Type 3 conformity certificate (per ISO/IEC 17067) from an SASO-accepted body based on a certified Product Safety Management System. Products must display the food contact symbol (Annex 3) and carry Arabic labeling including usage warnings and QR code for traceability. Products covered include plastics tableware and containers (HS 3924), wooden kitchen tools (HS 4419), paper and board products (HS 4811, 4823), ceramic and porcelain ware (HS 6911, 6912), nickel and aluminum utensils (HS 7508, 7615), tin-ware and metal closures (HS 8007, 8309), and manual kitchen tools and food processing machines (HS 8205, 8208, 8215, 8435, 8438).

---

name_ar: قائمة المواصفات المتعلقة بتطبيق اللائحة الفنية للمركبات ذاتية القيادة
summary:
This is the official SASO-approved list of standards relevant to implementing the Technical Regulation for Autonomous Vehicles (document reference م.إ-٢٠٩-٢٥-٠٤-٠٣-٠١), approved at SASO Board of Directors Meeting No. 209 on 22/01/1447H (17/07/2025). The list contains 171 standards spanning functional safety, automated driving systems, cybersecurity, structural safety, tyres, braking, lighting, emissions, and ICT/telematics. Compliance with these standards is deemed sufficient evidence of conformity with the essential requirements of the autonomous vehicles regulation. Manufacturers may adopt alternative technical solutions with SASO approval. The list is subject to periodic review and update.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Fire Fighting Equipment and Materials
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية لمعدات ومواد مكافحة الحرائق
summary:
This technical regulation (م.إ-172-19-04-02) issued by SASO establishes mandatory requirements for fire fighting equipment and materials sold in Saudi Arabia, covering intervention and extinguishing equipment, fire pumps, extinguishing materials, and their parts and accessories. Fire alarm and suppression systems are excluded. Suppliers must ensure products conform to the referenced SASO standards (Annex 1), obtain a conformity certificate from a SASO-accepted body via Type 3 assessment (product safety management system evaluation per ISO/IEC 17067), maintain a technical file including risk assessment and supplier declaration, and comply with labeling and packaging requirements. Market surveillance authorities and regulatory bodies have powers to sample, inspect, and withdraw non-compliant products. Non-compliance is subject to penalties under the Commercial Fraud Law.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Electronic Devices Used in Electronic Smoking Systems
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للأجهزة الإلكترونية المستخدمة في نظم التدخين الإلكتروني
summary:
This technical regulation (م.إ-172-19-04-03), issued by SASO and published November 2019, establishes mandatory safety, technical, and conformity assessment requirements for electronic devices used in electronic smoking systems (e-cigarettes, e-hookahs, vaping devices) under HS code 8543. Suppliers must obtain a national conformity certificate via the IECEE program from an SASO-accepted body and provide a supplier declaration of conformity. Products must comply with 13 referenced IEC/ISO/CISPR/GSO standards covering electrical safety, battery safety, EMC, and hazardous substance restrictions. Labeling must be in Arabic and/or English and include technical specs, traceability QR codes, and health warnings per SFDA requirements. Liquid/tobacco refill packaging is subject to SFDA requirements. Non-compliant products are subject to market withdrawal, recall, and penalties under applicable Saudi laws.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Motorcycles
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للدراجات الآلية
summary:
This technical regulation (م.إ-١٧٣-١٩-٠٤-٠٢) issued by SASO establishes mandatory safety and administrative requirements for motorcycles (الدراجات الآلية) sold in Saudi Arabia, covering categories UTV, ATV, L1eB, L3eA, L5eA, and L2eP including their electric equivalents. The regulation covers combustion engine and electric motorcycles used on public roads and off-road. Excluded products include three-wheeled cargo motorcycles (Toktok), slingshot vehicles, certain used motorcycles, and children's toy vehicles. Suppliers must obtain a Gulf Conformity Certificate for combustion engine motorcycles, or a SASO conformity certificate for electric motorcycles, per model. A technical file including supplier declaration, risk assessment, and Gulf registration documents must accompany products. The regulation references 77 standards covering safety, emissions, noise, braking, lighting, tires, VIN, and electric propulsion. Market surveillance and regulatory authorities have powers to sample, test, and withdraw non-compliant products. Penalties follow the commercial fraud prevention system.

---

name_en: Technical Regulation for Trailers and Semi-Trailers
name_ar: اللائحة الفنية للمقطورات ونصف المقطورات
summary:
This technical regulation establishes mandatory requirements for all trailers and semi-trailers (HS 8716) manufactured locally or imported into Saudi Arabia, excluding military-specific variants. It covers technical requirements for dimensions/weights, VIN numbering, lighting and reflectors, towing systems, underrun protection barriers, and general safety.
